header_normalization: trim, collapse internal whitespace/newlines to a single space, uppercase
source_file_reference: JUNIOR KILLER MAR'24 TO MAY'26 SALE REPORT.xlsb, sheet 'Sheet1'

validation_rules.is_return_when: quantity < 0 OR mrp_value < 0 (real data has both return conventions)
validation_rules.divide_by_zero_guard: if mrp_value == 0, discount_pct is null, not an error
notes: Same file family/layout as KILLER's menswear export (same column vocabulary, minor header-text variants -- e.g. DISTRIBUTOR NAME here vs DISTRIBUTOR NAME NEW for Killer, NAME AS PER REPORT RECEIVED here vs PARTY NAME (AS PER STORE SIGNAGE) for Killer), but a genuinely distinct brand code and store-code namespace (JKESIS### vs ESIS###) -- kept as its own (brand, product_line) config rather than sharing Killer's, per the frozen (brand, product_line)-keyed mapping design.

No F. YEAR column at all in this file (unlike Killer) -- financial_year is simply unmapped; not a problem since it isn't in required_canonical_fields and the analytics MVs derive financial_year from dim_calendar via sale_date, never from this raw supplied column.
STORE CODE values are prefixed JK (e.g. JKESIS011) -- distinct from Killer's ESIS### -- consistent with store_code being unique only within a brand (frozen decision), not globally.

product_line is 'kids' per the frozen decision's anticipated womenswear/kids/footwear expansion -- BRAND column value is literally 'JR KILLER' in every sampled row, confirming this is the kids line, not adult menswear.
